steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Words in Context question. The blank must complete the clause '______ astronauts to maintain the telescope can be expensive.' The word 'as' signals a causal explanation: the blank must describe the relationship with astronauts that makes maintenance costly.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘straightening’: 'Straightening astronauts' has no coherent meaning in context — it would mean physically aligning them, which is irrelevant to cost. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘forgetting about’: 'Forgetting about astronauts' inverts the logic: the text is about continuing to depend on astronauts, not about neglecting them. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘relying on’: 'Relying on astronauts' means depending on them for regular maintenance missions — and that ongoing dependence is exactly what makes the process expensive. Robots would eliminate this dependency. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘reducing’: 'Reducing astronauts' most naturally means lowering them in status; nothing in the text suggests this or why it would be expensive. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
